objective: Buildings are not only designed for energy savings but also for cooling; installed air-conditioning systems, cool roofs, double facades.

planned_budget_and_sources: Municipal funds

measure_name: Feasibility study and next phase for the deployment of the smart readiness indicator
objective: Assess readiness of the Lithuanian building sector for smart technologies, identify technical/financial options and prepare for full implementation.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Improved understanding of smart functions in Lithuanian buildings and identification of options and technology gaps
expected_impact: Assess the state of preparedness of the Lithuanian building sector for smart technologies

measure_name: EV-ready parking (>=20%) in new or refurbished non-residential buildings
objective: In new non-residential buildings under construction, refurbishment or overhaul, at least 20% of the total number of car parking spaces shall be capable of recharging electric vehicles.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Adaptation of at least 20% of car parking spaces in new or renovated buildings to electric-vehicle charging
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Pre-cabling requirement (>=50%) in new/refurbished non-residential buildings
objective: Add to STR 2.06.04:2014 that new/refurbished non-residential buildings must have at least 50% of parking spaces pre-cabled, and the remaining 50% ready for installation of recharging infrastructure.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Ensure >=50% pre-cabled parking spaces; remaining 50% ready for recharging infrastructure
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Bicycle parking in non-residential projects (>=15% capacity)
objective: Paragraph 17b of STR 2.06.04:2014 to be supplemented so every new building or reconstruction is equipped with bicycle parking spaces.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Install bicycle parking representing >=15% of the total capacity of non-residential occupants; include spaces for non-standard/large bicycles
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Office buildings: minimum EV recharging points
objective: New office buildings with >5 parking spaces and office buildings undergoing major renovation to be equipped with at least 1 recharging point for every two parking spaces.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Install >=1 recharging point per 2 parking spaces (areas with >5 parking spaces)
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Non-residential buildings (>=20 spaces): EV recharging/ducting
objective: Install >=1 recharging point for every 10 parking spaces or ducting infrastructure in all non-residential buildings with >20 parking spaces.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: 10% of parking spaces equipped with recharging points or all spaces ready with ducting infrastructure
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Public bodies' buildings: pre-cabling (>=50%)
objective: At least 50% of public bodies' buildings (or buildings occupied by them) have >=50% pre-cabling of parking spaces.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: 50% of parking spaces pre-cabled
entities_responsible: AM, Construction Inspectorate, Municipalities, Construction Operators

measure_name: Fire safety rules in car parks
objective: Rules on fire safety in car parks: Fire Safety Rules for Car Storage Facilities, approved by Order No 1-44 of the Director of the Fire and Rescue Department.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote advanced technologies and infrastructures for sustainable mobility in buildings
quantitative_target: Ensure implementation of fire safety requirements in all newly constructed or refurbished parking areas
planned_budget_and_sources: Funds for project developers
entities_responsible: MoI, MoI Fire & Rescue Department, Construction Inspectorate

measure_name: MT10-E Ensure demand for energy sector professionals (heat, electricity, RES, etc.) and the promotion of the energy profession in Lithuania
objective: Develop and communicate lists of new professions in the energy sector; create a rolling programme to promote energy studies; increase the attractiveness and modernity of energy-oriented study programmes.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to increase public awareness and training of professionals

measure_name: H5 Mainstreaming climate change in all education systems
objective: Ensure that education related to climate change is included in all levels of education and higher education programmes in order to bring about a change in societal behaviour.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to increase public awareness and training of professionals

measure_name: EE4-E Agreements with energy suppliers on consumer education and advice
objective: Measure to implement and promote agreements with energy suppliers and consumers on changing behaviour and choosing solutions that improve energy efficiency
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
quantitative_target: Renovate 5,042 multi-apartment buildings
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funding: EUR 3,004.11 million; Public funding: EUR 1,152.89 million (Public funds)
expected_impact: Save up to 248.96 kt of GHG and about 2,773.21 GWh of total fuel & energy

measure_name: RES20-P Encouraging electricity consumers to choose energy from RES
objective: Engage energy consumers more in a greener consumption model and shape their behaviour to reduce energy consumption
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
quantitative_target: Reach 119,395 people with an education campaign
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funding: EUR 3,237.4 million; Public funding: EUR 1,047.4 million (Public funds)

measure_name: EN4-P Information for hard-to-reach (not Internet-enabled) consumers on compensation and energy savings
objective: Provide information to hard-to-reach consumers on compensation and energy savings
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funds: EUR 122 million; Public funds: EUR 2 million (Public funds)

measure_name: EN5-P Create an information hub
objective: The information hub will provide information on compensation, connection to renewable energy communities, energy efficiency, building renovation and energy savings
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funds: EUR 122 million; Public funds: EUR 2 million (Public funds)

measure_name: Public information on household (housing) heating
objective: Information campaigns providing important material and expert advice to citizens to familiarise themselves with: the health effects of solid fuel and waste incineration; environmental impacts; obligations and possibilities for the proper operation and maintenance of boilers/chimneys; opportunities to install cost-effective and energy-efficient domestic heating systems; potential for improving building energy efficiency; energy efficiency measures and solutions
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
entities_responsible: SAM, NVSC, DGEF, Institute of Hygiene, ENMIN, Municipalities
expected_impact: More conscious consumer behaviour, switching to less polluting fuels, and improving the efficiency of installations

measure_name: Renovation map of Lithuania
objective: Extend the integrated building renovation map to monitor, analyse and forecast the renovation processes of all buildings at national level. Objective: efficient data collection, analysis and planning of measures (data aggregation, system integration, GIS, reporting module).
type_of_policy_or_measure: Awareness-raising and campaigning measures
quantitative_target: Create an active map reflecting 100% of relevant buildings and their refurbishment procedures (status)
planned_budget_and_sources: State budget (State budget)

measure_name: L19-E Promotion of organic construction
objective: The first part covers the implementation of pilot building refurbishment projects using standardised modular structures made of organic materials, and the development of recommendations for wider application of the solutions. Activities occur 2023-2025 and lead to an average 30% primary energy reduction. The second part supports deployment of production capacities of standardised modular structures or organic materials needed to achieve the Long-term Renovation Strategy objectives.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote modular and industrialised solutions for construction and building renovation
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funding: EUR 598.19 million; Public funding: EUR 561.83 million (Public funds)

measure_name: L12-P Promotion of organic construction
objective: Promote the refurbishment of 860 multi-apartment buildings using standardised modular structures made of organic materials.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote modular and industrialised solutions for construction and building renovation
quantitative_target: Refurbishment of 860 multi-apartment buildings
planned_budget_and_sources: Total funding: EUR 122 million and public funding of EUR 2 million (Public funds)

measure_name: EE2-P Renovation (modernisation) of multi-apartment buildings using modular structures
objective: Out of all 860 multi-apartment buildings planned for renovation, modular building products (liquids) produced in factories from renewable natural resources of organic origin will be used.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote modular and industrialised solutions for construction and building renovation
quantitative_target: Refurbish 860 multi-apartment buildings using panels
planned_budget_and_sources: Not separately foreseen (co-financing under 3.3 for measure EE2-P) (Public + Private funds)

measure_name: Regulatory base for modular and industrialised construction
objective: Supplement the existing Construction Act and STR documents on modular & industrialised construction (covering building design, manufacturing, transport, assembly, permit processes) in line with the Directive, Annex I and EC Report Guidance.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Measures to promote modular and industrialised solutions for construction and building renovation
source: Directive, Annex I; EC Report Guidance
quantitative_target: 20% increase in the use of modular technologies (in construction & refurbishment) and decrease in energy consumption
planned_budget_and_sources: State budget
expected_impact: 20 modular and industrial projects per year
